name: JD Vance
summary: JD Vance has consistently demonstrated a pattern of behavior that is divisive, discriminatory, and dismissive of facts. He has repeatedly expressed disdain for childless individuals, women, and marginalized groups, using derogatory language and promoting policies that would harm them. Vance has also shown a willingness to spread misinformation and conspiracy theories, including claims about Haitian migrants and the 2020 presidential election. Additionally, he has used his interpretation of Catholic theology to justify his support for policies that are harmful to migrants and other vulnerable groups. Vance's actions and statements suggest a strong authoritarian streak, as evidenced by his advice to fire all civil servants and replace them with Trump supporters, and his endorsement of a book that praises fascist dictators.

entries.fact: JD Vance complained about 'highly educated transplants' in Silicon Valley in a New York Times op-ed.
JD Vance said elite tech crowds wielded 'political-financial power in combination with a certain condescension'.
Our Ohio Renewal, a nonprofit founded by JD Vance, spent more on 'management services' than on programs to fight opioid abuse in its first year.

Our Ohio Renewal, a nonprofit founded by JD Vance, sent psychiatrist Sally Satel to Ohio's Appalachian region for a yearlong residency, which was 'tainted' by ties between Satel, her employer, and Purdue Pharma.
JD Vance was baptized and confirmed in the Catholic Church, and later used his interpretation of Catholic theology to justify his support for policies like the deportation of migrants
Our Ohio Renewal, a nonprofit founded by JD Vance, closed with sparse achievements.

JD Vance said in a speech at a Center for Christian Virtue leadership meeting that childless teachers were 'trying to brainwash the minds of our children' and criticized American Federation of Teachers President Randi Weingarten
JD Vance suggested in an interview on The Charlie Kirk Show that childless people should be taxed at a higher rate than those with children

JD Vance said in a Fox News interview that the country is run by 'a bunch of childless cat ladies who are miserable at their own lives and the choices that they've made and so they want to make the rest of the country miserable too'
JD Vance voted against raising the debt ceiling, standing against final passage of the Fiscal Responsibility Act of 2023
JD Vance and Representative Marjorie Taylor Greene introduced legislation that would have made gender-affirming care for minors a federal crime, with penalties of up to 12 years in prison
JD Vance expressed a belief that childlessness is linked to sociopathy and suggested parents should have more voting power than non-parents.
JD Vance sponsored the Dismantle DEI Act, which would ban federal diversity, equity, and inclusion programs and funding for agencies, contractors, and organizations receiving federal funds
JD Vance said that Vice President Kamala Harris 'has been a bad vice president' in an interview.

JD Vance told a private fundraiser that the 'bad news is that Kamala Harris does not have the same baggage as Joe Biden ... Kamala Harris is obviously not struggling in the same ways that Joe Biden did'.
JD Vance said that Trump had 'said that explicitly that he would' veto a national abortion ban.
JD Vance proposed federal criminalization of gender-affirming care for minors.

JD Vance said that Kamala Harris 'can go to hell' because 'she wants to yell at Donald Trump because he showed up' at Arlington National Cemetery, despite Harris not having publicly discussed the incident.
JD Vance said 'Don't do this fake outrage thing' in response to criticism of Trump's visit to Arlington National Cemetery.

JD Vance defended his claims about Haitian migrants eating cats, saying that he was willing 'to create stories so that the American media actually pays attention ... we're creating a story, meaning we're creating the American media focusing on it.'

JD Vance alleged that Haitian illegal immigrants were 'draining social services and generally causing chaos all over Springfield, Ohio' and that 'reports now show that people have had their pets abducted and eaten by people who shouldn't be in this country'.

JD Vance used the death of a child in Springfield, Ohio, to claim that the child was 'murdered by a Haitian migrant who had no right to be here', despite the child's death being the result of an accidental collision between vehicles
JD Vance said he did not believe Trump lost the 2020 presidential election and that he believed 'Big Tech rigged the election' through censorship

JD Vance objected to fact-checking during the vice presidential debate, saying 'The rules were that you were not going to fact check', and argued that Haitian immigrants in Springfield, Ohio should not be considered legal because the federal government decided their protected status after they had arrived in the United States.
JD Vance cast the tie-breaking vote to confirm Pete Hegseth as Secretary of Defense.
JD Vance wrote that 'judges aren't allowed to control the executive's legitimate power' after multiple federal judges issued temporary rulings against various Trump administration actions.

JD Vance told Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy that 'it's disrespectful for you to come to the Oval Office and try to litigate this in front of the American media' during a meeting at the White House.

JD Vance incorrectly claimed that Zelenskyy 'went to Pennsylvania and campaigned for the opposition in October' 2024, when in fact Zelenskyy had visited a factory to thank workers producing ammunition for Ukraine.

JD Vance visited Greenland and said 'We can't just ignore the president's desires' for the U.S. to acquire Greenland, and warned that Greenland faced the 'encroachment of powerful countries' China and Russia 'as they expand their ambitions'.

JD Vance lamented that the 'globalist economy' had caused the United States to 'borrow money from Chinese peasants to buy the things those Chinese peasants manufacture', and China slammed his remarks as 'unacceptable'.
JD Vance advised Trump to fire all civil servants and replace them with Trump supporters
J.D. Vance argued against the need for rape and incest exceptions in abortion laws
JD Vance was criticized by Pope Francis for misrepresenting Church teaching in his support for policies like the deportation of migrants
J.D. Vance stated he would not support federal legislation to codify marriage equality and is against same-sex marriage
JD Vance said he would not have certified the 2020 presidential election results if he had been vice president, and suggested some states Trump lost should have sent pro-Trump electors to Washington
JD Vance called for 'a de-woke-ification program' even if the courts say it is illegal
J.D. Vance proposed a federal ban on gender transition care for minors
JD Vance made a comment referring to some women as 'childless cat ladies', sparking widespread criticism
J.D. Vance suggested that parents should have a bigger say in democracy than non-parents
J.D. Vance previously hyped a plan to give parents more votes
JD Vance defended his 'childless cat ladies' comment, describing it as 'sarcastic'
JD Vance stated that 'childless people' in US leadership are 'more sociopathic'
J.D. Vance endorsed a book that calls far-left individuals 'unhumans' and praises fascist dictators
JD Vance told Kamala Harris to 'go to hell' in response to criticism she did not actually make
JD Vance appeared at an event hosted by a hard-right Christian nationalist who had previously spoken about Kamala Harris's 'witchcraft'
JD Vance stated that Donald Trump did not lose the 2020 election, saying 'not by the words I would use'
JD Vance's cousin, Nate Vance, criticized him for his stance on Ukraine, accusing him and Trump of aiding Russia
